items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. Adobe has also released an isolated patch for these issues.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A high-severity vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) due to improper encoding or escaping of output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution. Patches were released on May 25, 2026, and CISA has added it to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution. While Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities that could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends applying security patches as quickly as possible.
